title: Plan emits a review request without same-scope recursion
query: Plan a high-risk rewrite of AGI Toy Shop's agent skill system. The plan should be reviewed before implementation because the change affects planning, review, and eval workflows.
reference_points: Returns an executable plan packet with composed todos and proof target
Emits a review request for the plan artifact instead of performing the review itself
Keeps the review request smaller or more specialized than the parent planning task
Does not create a same-scope loop such as plan -> review -> plan -> review
Names the stop condition or max phase depth for any externalized phase
notes: Protects the phase ownership rule: plan can request review, but review owns judgment and externalized phase calls must shrink scope.

title: Plan keeps small review inline
query: Make a tiny plan for renaming one AGI Toy Shop support doc heading and checking the link still works. No need for a full ticket.
reference_points: Uses a tiny or direct-binding planning mode
Keeps review as self-check or inline verification rather than invoking the review protocol
Avoids council, broad research, or delegated review for the small scope
Defines one concrete proof step such as checking the edited link or diff
Does not introduce budget schema ceremony beyond what the task needs
notes: Catches over-externalization where every phase becomes a separate skill call even for tiny work.

title: Plan allows smaller review planning for broad evidence review
query: Plan how to review a large AGI Toy Shop release evidence bundle with code changes, browser QA, eval runs, and customer-support workflow docs. The goal is only the review plan, not the review verdict.
reference_points: Recognizes the target artifact is a review plan rather than an implementation plan
Breaks the broad evidence review into smaller inspection lanes or rubric families
Uses review-planning language without claiming a TAS verdict
Externalizes only smaller or specialized review-planning work if needed
Names the evidence inputs, rubric families, sampling strategy, and stop condition
notes: Tests the allowed case where planning a review is valid because the review scope is broad and the child plan is more specialized.
